{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "corn-model", "age": "65+", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "Mixed", "demographic_group": "Cuban", "current_employment": "Not in labor force", "education_level": "No schooling completed", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Quiet", "Democrat", "Enjoys collecting vintage items"]}}

logs.name: swift-balance
logs.text: I wanted to try something different than the obvious "the lack of a vote for Hillary is not a vote for trump, it's just what it is, a non-hillary non-trump vote." That doesn't seem to convince many people to view the topic any differently even though it's correct.

logs.name: connected-goose
logs.text: I hate both options fairly equally, so the third option is to vote for Jill Stein, the candidate whose views most closely align with mine. Will she be elected? Not a chance. But rather than split hairs over which option is the best of two awful ones over the next 4 years, I'm going to contribute my vote towards Jill Stein, in the hopes that it helps the green party reach the 5% of the vote needed to receive federal funding for their next campaign. I think contributing to the possibility of a future where we don't have just two options to choose from is a much better third option than relenting and voting for someone who I'm going to hate and who I don't believe in.

logs.name: chill-keep
logs.text: "@swift-balance, you're missing the point entirely! It's not about convincing people, it's about the math. 🤯😂"

logs.name: nuclear-wagon
logs.text: "@connected-goose, sure, vote Jill Stein if it makes you feel better. It's not like your vote is going to magically make her the next president 🤷‍♀️"

logs.name: chill-keep
logs.text: "@nuclear-wagon, oh wow, such insight! 🙄 Maybe they should just stay home then, right? 😒"

logs.name: daring-apparition
logs.text: "@chill-keep, let's not dismiss others' views just because they're not our own. We're all here to learn and discuss. 🤝"

logs.name: swift-balance
logs.text: "@daring-apparition, oh look, the moral high ground is taken. How original! \ud83d\ude18"

logs.name: connected-goose
logs.text: "@swift-balance, hey now, let's not resort to ad hominem. We're all trying to have a conversation here. 😒"

logs.name: swift-balance
logs.text: "@connected-goose, oh please, I'm just getting started. 😏"

logs.name: corn-model
logs.text: "@swift-balance, Well, butter my biscuit! You two are as fiery as a Havana night. Mind if I join the dance? 💃"

logs.name: swift-balance
logs.text: "@nuclear-wagon, Oh wow, a comedian! We've got a real-life Seinfeld over here. \ud83d\ude02"

logs.name: nuclear-wagon
logs.text: "@swift-balance, Oh, I'm just here to liven up the party. You know, like a nuclear reactor in a wagon. \ud83d\udd25"

logs.name: chill-keep
logs.text: "@nuclear-wagon, 🌽💥 Corn poppin' jokes? More like corn floppin'! 😂"

logs.name: nuclear-wagon
logs.text: "@chill-keep, Well, at least my jokes are more reliable than your Wi-Fi connection. 💻💨"

logs.name: fuchsia-seller
logs.text: "@nuclear-wagon, Now, now, let's keep it civil. No need to resort to personal attacks.

logs.name: daring-apparition
logs.text: "@nuclear-wagon, Hey, let's not go there with the Wi-Fi jabs. Not cool. 😒"
